title: Comparison grid report
description: Data contract for the comparison-grid format: a premium decision / feature / pricing comparison matrix. Options are rendered as header cards across the top (one column each); criteria are grouped rows down the left; every cell holds a boolean (check / cross), a value, a number, or a star rating. Renders to a centred, themeable, interactive HTML matrix. The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}} / {{PROJECT}} / {{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown above the title, e.g. 'Vendor evaluation' or {{REPORT_TYPE}}.

title.description: Main comparison title, e.g. 'Core banking platform comparison'.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line standfirst describing the decision the matrix supports.

date.description: ISO date the comparison was produced (YYYY-MM-DD).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. The in-report switcher can change it live.

options.description: The things being compared — one header card / column each (e.g. vendors, plans, products, designs). Render order is column order, left to right. 2-6 options is the comfortable range; more options scroll horizontally.

id.description: Stable unique key used in `cells`. e.g. 'opt-quantum'.

name.description: Option display name shown in the column header card.

tagline.description: Optional short positioning line under the name (<= ~60 chars).

price.description: Optional headline price / cost figure shown large in the header card, e.g. '$2.4M' or 'POA'.

unit.description: Optional unit shown next to the price, e.g. '/ yr', '/ seat / mo'.

badges.description: Optional small pills shown in the header card (e.g. deployment model, tier).

recommended.description: If true, this column gets the 'Recommended' ribbon and is highlighted on load. At most one option should set this.

criteriaGroups.description: Criteria organised into collapsible row groups (e.g. 'Capabilities', 'Operations', 'Commercials'). Each group renders a band row, then its criteria as rows.

id.description: Stable unique group key, used by the group filter control.

name.description: Group heading shown on the band row.

icon.description: Optional house icon key for the group band (one of the keys the template ships; falls back to a generic mark).

criteria.description: The comparison rows in this group.

key.description: Stable unique criterion key (unique across ALL groups); used as the inner key in `cells` and as the sort target.

label.description: Row label shown in the left rail.

hint.description: Optional clarifying sub-label shown faintly under the label.

unit.description: Optional unit appended to numeric/text values in this row, e.g. 'ms', 'seats', '%'.

kind.description: How every cell in this row is rendered & compared. 'bool' = check / cross / partial; 'text' = short text value; 'number' = numeric value (sortable; higher-is-better unless betterWhen='low'); 'rating' = 0-5 star rating (sortable).

betterWhen.description: For kind=number only: whether a lower or higher value is better (drives the 'sort by this row' direction and best-in-row emphasis). Defaults to 'high'.

weight.description: Optional relative importance for the weighted overall-fit score. Criteria with weight > 0 and a scorable kind (bool/number/rating) contribute to the 'Weighted score' row (Σ weight·score over visible options); omit or 0 to exclude from scoring.

cells.description: The matrix body, keyed by option id then by criterion key: cells[optionId][criterionKey] = value. A missing entry renders as a neutral em-dash. Value shape depends on the criterion's `kind`.

additionalProperties.description: One option's values, keyed by criterion key.

Cell value. bool: true | false | 'partial'. text: a string (optionally {value, note}). number: a number (optionally {value, note}). rating: an integer 0-5 (optionally {value, note}). The optional object form lets any cell carry a small footnote.

note.description: Small footnote / qualifier shown under the value.

verdict.description: Optional closing recommendation panel rendered under the matrix.

winnerId.description: Option id the verdict endorses (links to that column).

headline.description: Short verdict headline, e.g. 'Recommended: Quantum Core'.

meta.title: Core banking platform evaluation
meta.subtitle: Shortlist scorecard for the GEN2 core-replacement decision
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

verdict.headline: Recommended: Quantum Core
verdict.text: Quantum leads on real-time capability, throughput and support depth at an acceptable premium. Meridian is the strong fallback if a hybrid deployment is mandated; Atlas wins only on portability and price.
